title: The Valley
content:
I will walk down to the valley
    And lay my head in her breast,
    Where are two white doves,
    The Queen of Love's,
    In a silken nest;
    And, all the afternoon,
    They croon and croon
    The one word "Rest!"
    And a little stream
    That runs thereby
    Sings "Dream!"
    Over and over
    It sings - 
    "O lover,
    Dream!"
meaning:
Richard Le Gallienne's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Valley"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
